Tottori Line)|ja|メリーバード号 (広島 - 鳥取線)|12=draft}} operate. == Footnotes and References == {{Reflist |refs= *[http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf Tottori Prefecture Cultural Tourism Bureau, Tourism Policy Division: Visitor Dynamics Survey (Heisei 22)] (accessed September 9, 2014). * [http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226511/3003onsenirikomi.pdf Tottori Prefecture Tourism Exchange Bureau, Tourism Strategy Division: Hot Spring Visitor Numbers (March, Heisei 30)] (accessed June 7, 2018). *『全国温泉大事典』, pp.560–563 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』, pp.492–500 "Sekigane-machi" *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（角川日本地名大辞典）』, p.436 "Sekigane Onsen" *『全國温泉案内』, pp.443–453 *『溫泉案内』, pp.216–227 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』, pp.334–335 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』, pp.251–252 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』, pp.158–160 *[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財等データベース: Wooden Jizō Bosatsu Half-seated Statue] (accessed September 25, 2014). }} == See Also == * [[Tourist Attractions in Tottori Prefecture]] * [[Misaso Onsen]] – Another radioactive hot spring nearby == External Links == * [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] * [https://www.sekigane-yurari.com Tottori Sekigane Onsen Yurarī] {{Tottori Prefecture Hot Springs}} {{citation needed|せきかねおんせん}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kurayoshi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] [[Category:Pages that attribution was given to on Feb 22]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sekigane Onsen]] [[ja:関金温泉]] [[ja:有馬温泉]] [[ja:増富温泉]] [[ja:玉川温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ja:母畑温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sekigane Onsen}}

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Sekigane Onsen |image = File:Sekigane onsen Yumeikan.jpg |Comment = Sekigane Onsen Yumemyōkan |Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 21 |Latitude Seconds = 44.97 |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 45 |Longitude Seconds = 46.21 |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|12=draft}} Sekigane-machi, Sekigane-yado |Access = : By car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原IC|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft}} toward Kurayoshi
: By rail and local bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|12=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|12=draft}} for about 35 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Radiation Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft}} |Temperature = 40 - 62 |Discharge = 337 L/min |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 5 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 23,410 |Statistical Year = 2017 |External links = [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] |Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 21 | lat_sec = 44.97 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 45 | lon_sec = 46.21 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Sekigane Onsen | position = bottom | mark = Map pointer.svg | marksize = 9 | width = 300 | float = right | caption = Location of Sekigane Onsen | alt = Location of Sekigane Onsen }} '''Sekigane Onsen''' (せきがねおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|12=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft}}). == Water Quality == It is a {{ill|radiation hot spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft}}, ranking second in Japan after {{ill|Misaso Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=draft}} in its content of {{ill|radon|ja|ラドン|12=draft}} and other radioactive elements. Its water is colorless and odorless, renowned as a "beautiful hot spring." Although the reason why both Sekigane Onsen and Misaso Onsen exhibit radioactivity was long unknown, it was determined in 1955 (Showa 30) that a water-formed {{ill|uranium|ja|ウラン|12=draft}} mineral deposit discovered nearby is the source. The source temperature is between 40 and 62℃. == Onsen Town == {{File:Sekigane onsen YURARI.jpg|thumb|Sekigane Onsen Yurarī}} The hot spring town extends along the eastern foothills of {{ill|Daisen|ja|大山|12=draft}}, located deep from {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft}}. There are five ryokan in the area. One public bath, "Kan no Yu," is available, and as a day-use facility, "Sekigane Yumemyōkan" is also in operation. The town comprises both an old onsen district and a new district developed in 1963 (Showa 38) that draws water from a municipal source. When the area was designated as a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}} on March 24, 1969 (Showa 45), local regulations were enacted to centralize management of the hot spring sources. An old national lodging facility, Green School Sekigane, once existed here. It has been renovated and reopened as {{ill|HOTEL Hoshitori Terrace Sekigane|ja|HOTEL星取テラスせきがね|12=draft}} on April 26, 2025 (Reiwa 7)[https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/717807 Kurayoshi's old national lodging renewed; Sekigane hotel to open on April 26 with universal design adoption] (Shanin Chūō Shinpo) January 24, 2025.. == History == Various legends exist regarding the founding of the hot spring. Some sources attribute its opening to the Yōrō era (717–723 AD), others to the Enryaku era (782–806 AD) as noted in the Japanese Hot Spring Chronicles, while still others attribute it to the 12th year of the Jōgan era (870 AD) or even to the Genkō era (1331–1333 AD). One legend recounts that when a crane was seen bathing, the monk 【行基】 discovered the spring, and 【Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)】 later developed the desolate hot spring area. Some accounts even credit the discovery to Kūkai. According to the Genkō-era version, the lord of Sekigane Castle, Yamana Kotarō, is said to have opened the springYamana Kotarō was a member of the Yamana clan, guardians of Hōki, who was said to have been killed in battle at Sekigane during a succession dispute. However, this incident likely occurred in the late 15th century, which is significantly later than the Genkō era (mid-14th century).. Additionally, early Showa-era literature states that Gyōki (668–749) discovered the spring, and after a period of decline, Kūkai (774–835) revived it, with Yamana Kotarō (14??–1489?) installing the bath.. Sekigane Onsen is also known by the ancient names "Ginnoyu" and "Shiroganeyu" due to the beauty of its waters, and it appears as "Ginyu" in the Hōki Mindanki. During the Edo period, it was located along the 【Binchū Kaidō】 (also known as the Mimasaka Kaidō) connecting central Tottori with the Sanyō region, and a post station, Sekigane-juku, was established there. Prior to the era when radiation was measured, the water was described as a pale yellow, odorless carbonated spring. According to surveys conducted by the Tottori Prefecture based on the admission tax, the number of users peaked at around 34,000 in 2003 (Heisei 15). Usage then declined to approximately 13,000 in 2012, but has since rebounded to around 23,000 in 2017. Estimates based on municipal reports prior to 1997 (Heisei 9) suggest that during the bubble economy, annual usage might have been around 400,000 visitors. == Events and Festivals == In this region, traditional customs such as the imperial procession on October 7 and rice-planting songs (denoted in the "Daisenji Engi Emaki") are observed. * The "Sekigane Satomi Festival" is held on the first Sunday of September. == Nearby Attractions == * '''[[Daikatsuyama Jizōin (Kurayoshi)|Daikatsuyama Jizōin]]''' – Historically known as "Sekino Jizō" in Sekigane Onsen. The wooden half-seated Jizō Bosatsu statue here is designated as a nationally important cultural property, indicating that the Jizōin was established in 1192 (Kenkyu 3) by Sasaki Takatsuna. (Note that the current structure is a reconstruction.) Other cultural assets, such as a Giboju and a Song dynasty celadon incense burner, are also preserved. * {{ill|Yukase Shrine|ja|湯関神社|12=draft}} * The ruins of {{ill|Kamei Castle|ja|亀井城跡|12=draft}} == Access == * By Car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft}} toward Kurayoshi. * By Rail and Bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|12=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|12=draft}} for about 35 minutes. : Previously, a branch of the {{ill|Kurayoshi Line|ja|倉吉線|12=draft}} served Sekigane Station as the nearest stop, but the line was abolished on April 1, 1985 (Showa 60) and the station was removed. : Although bus schedules and frequencies are limited, it is also possible to access the area by taking a community bus ("Maniwakun") on the 37 "Harsan-Kūse Route" from the {{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|12=draft}}/{{ill|Chūgoku Katsuya Station|ja|中国勝山駅|12=draft}}, transferring at Miyata Stop onto the 32 "Chūso-Sekigane Route." * By Highway Bus: Services such as the {{ill|Shin-Kurayoshi Kaidō Express|ja|新倉吉街道エクスプレス|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Meribird-go (Hiroshima - Tottori Line)|ja|メリーバード号 (広島 - 鳥取線)|12=draft}} operate. == Footnotes and References == {{Reflist |refs= *[http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf Tottori Prefecture Cultural Tourism Bureau, Tourism Policy Division: Visitor Dynamics Survey (Heisei 22)] (accessed September 9, 2014). * [http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226511/3003onsenirikomi.pdf Tottori Prefecture Tourism Exchange Bureau, Tourism Strategy Division: Hot Spring Visitor Numbers (March, Heisei 30)] (accessed June 7, 2018). *『全国温泉大事典』, pp.560–563 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』, pp.492–500 "Sekigane-machi" *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（角川日本地名大辞典）』, p.436 "Sekigane Onsen" *『全國温泉案内』, pp.443–453 *『溫泉案内』, pp.216–227 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』, pp.334–335 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』, pp.251–252 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』, pp.158–160 *[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財等データベース: Wooden Jizō Bosatsu Half-seated Statue] (accessed September 25, 2014). }} == See Also == * [[Tourist Attractions in Tottori Prefecture]] * [[Misaso Onsen]] – Another radioactive hot spring nearby == External Links == * [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] * [https://www.sekigane-yurari.com Tottori Sekigane Onsen Yurarī] {{Tottori Prefecture Hot Springs}} {{citation needed|せきかねおんせん}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kurayoshi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] [[Category:Pages that attribution was given to on Feb 22]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sekigane Onsen]] [[ja:関金温泉]] [[ja:有馬温泉]] [[ja:増富温泉]] [[ja:玉川温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ja:母畑温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sekigane Onsen}}

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Sekigane Onsen |image = File:Sekigane onsen Yumeikan.jpg |Comment = Sekigane Onsen Yumemyōkan |Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 21 |Latitude Seconds = 44.97 |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 45 |Longitude Seconds = 46.21 |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|12=draft}} Sekigane-machi, Sekigane-yado |Access = : By car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原IC|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft}} toward Kurayoshi
: By rail and local bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|12=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|12=draft}} for about 35 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Radiation Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft}} |Temperature = 40 - 62 |Discharge = 337 L/min |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 5 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 23,410 |Statistical Year = 2017 |External links = [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] |Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 21 | lat_sec = 44.97 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 45 | lon_sec = 46.21 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Sekigane Onsen | position = bottom | mark = Map pointer.svg | marksize = 9 | width = 300 | float = right | caption = Location of Sekigane Onsen | alt = Location of Sekigane Onsen }} '''Sekigane Onsen''' (せきがねおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|12=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft}}). == Water Quality == It is a {{ill|radiation hot spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft}}, ranking second in Japan after {{ill|Misaso Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=draft}} in its content of {{ill|radon|ja|ラドン|12=draft}} and other radioactive elements. Its water is colorless and odorless, renowned as a "beautiful hot spring." Although the reason why both Sekigane Onsen and Misaso Onsen exhibit radioactivity was long unknown, it was determined in 1955 (Showa 30) that a water-formed {{ill|uranium|ja|ウラン|12=draft}} mineral deposit discovered nearby is the source. The source temperature is between 40 and 62℃. == Onsen Town == {{File:Sekigane onsen YURARI.jpg|thumb|Sekigane Onsen Yurarī}} The hot spring town extends along the eastern foothills of {{ill|Daisen|ja|大山|12=draft}}, located deep from {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft}}. There are five ryokan in the area. One public bath, "Kan no Yu," is available, and as a day-use facility, "Sekigane Yumemyōkan" is also in operation. The town comprises both an old onsen district and a new district developed in 1963 (Showa 38) that draws water from a municipal source. When the area was designated as a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}} on March 24, 1969 (Showa 45), local regulations were enacted to centralize management of the hot spring sources. An old national lodging facility, Green School Sekigane, once existed here. It has been renovated and reopened as {{ill|HOTEL Hoshitori Terrace Sekigane|ja|HOTEL星取テラスせきがね|12=draft}} on April 26, 2025 (Reiwa 7)[https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/717807 Kurayoshi's old national lodging renewed; Sekigane hotel to open on April 26 with universal design adoption] (Shanin Chūō Shinpo) January 24, 2025.. == History == Various legends exist regarding the founding of the hot spring. Some sources attribute its opening to the Yōrō era (717–723 AD), others to the Enryaku era (782–806 AD) as noted in the Japanese Hot Spring Chronicles, while still others attribute it to the 12th year of the Jōgan era (870 AD) or even to the Genkō era (1331–1333 AD). One legend recounts that when a crane was seen bathing, the monk 【行基】 discovered the spring, and 【Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)】 later developed the desolate hot spring area. Some accounts even credit the discovery to Kūkai. According to the Genkō-era version, the lord of Sekigane Castle, Yamana Kotarō, is said to have opened the springYamana Kotarō was a member of the Yamana clan, guardians of Hōki, who was said to have been killed in battle at Sekigane during a succession dispute. However, this incident likely occurred in the late 15th century, which is significantly later than the Genkō era (mid-14th century).. Additionally, early Showa-era literature states that Gyōki (668–749) discovered the spring, and after a period of decline, Kūkai (774–835) revived it, with Yamana Kotarō (14??–1489?) installing the bath.. Sekigane Onsen is also known by the ancient names "Ginnoyu" and "Shiroganeyu" due to the beauty of its waters, and it appears as "Ginyu" in the Hōki Mindanki. During the Edo period, it was located along the 【Binchū Kaidō】 (also known as the Mimasaka Kaidō) connecting central Tottori with the Sanyō region, and a post station, Sekigane-juku, was established there. Prior to the era when radiation was measured, the water was described as a pale yellow, odorless carbonated spring. According to surveys conducted by the Tottori Prefecture based on the admission tax, the number of users peaked at around 34,000 in 2003 (Heisei 15). Usage then declined to approximately 13,000 in 2012, but has since rebounded to around 23,000 in 2017. Estimates based on municipal reports prior to 1997 (Heisei 9) suggest that during the bubble economy, annual usage might have been around 400,000 visitors. == Events and Festivals == In this region, traditional customs such as the imperial procession on October 7 and rice-planting songs (denoted in the "Daisenji Engi Emaki") are observed. * The "Sekigane Satomi Festival" is held on the first Sunday of September. == Nearby Attractions == * '''[[Daikatsuyama Jizōin (Kurayoshi)|Daikatsuyama Jizōin]]''' – Historically known as "Sekino Jizō" in Sekigane Onsen. The wooden half-seated Jizō Bosatsu statue here is designated as a nationally important cultural property, indicating that the Jizōin was established in 1192 (Kenkyu 3) by Sasaki Takatsuna. (Note that the current structure is a reconstruction.) Other cultural assets, such as a Giboju and a Song dynasty celadon incense burner, are also preserved. * {{ill|Yukase Shrine|ja|湯関神社|12=draft}} * The ruins of {{ill|Kamei Castle|ja|亀井城跡|12=draft}} == Access == * By Car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft}} toward Kurayoshi. * By Rail and Bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|12=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|12=draft}} for about 35 minutes. : Previously, a branch of the {{ill|Kurayoshi Line|ja|倉吉線|12=draft}} served Sekigane Station as the nearest stop, but the line was abolished on April 1, 1985 (Showa 60) and the station was removed. : Although bus schedules and frequencies are limited, it is also possible to access the area by taking a community bus ("Maniwakun") on the 37 "Harsan-Kūse Route" from the {{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|12=draft}}/{{ill|Chūgoku Katsuya Station|ja|中国勝山駅|12=draft}}, transferring at Miyata Stop onto the 32 "Chūso-Sekigane Route." * By Highway Bus: Services such as the {{ill|Shin-Kurayoshi Kaidō Express|ja|新倉吉街道エクスプレス|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Meribird-go (Hiroshima - Tottori Line)|ja|メリーバード号 (広島 - 鳥取線)|12=draft}} operate. == Footnotes and References == {{Reflist |refs= *[http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf Tottori Prefecture Cultural Tourism Bureau, Tourism Policy Division: Visitor Dynamics Survey (Heisei 22)] (accessed September 9, 2014). * [http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226511/3003onsenirikomi.pdf Tottori Prefecture Tourism Exchange Bureau, Tourism Strategy Division: Hot Spring Visitor Numbers (March, Heisei 30)] (accessed June 7, 2018). *『全国温泉大事典』, pp.560–563 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』, pp.492–500 "Sekigane-machi" *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（角川日本地名大辞典）』, p.436 "Sekigane Onsen" *『全國温泉案内』, pp.443–453 *『溫泉案内』, pp.216–227 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』, pp.334–335 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』, pp.251–252 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』, pp.158–160 *[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財等データベース: Wooden Jizō Bosatsu Half-seated Statue] (accessed September 25, 2014). }} == See Also == * [[Tourist Attractions in Tottori Prefecture]] * [[Misaso Onsen]] – Another radioactive hot spring nearby == External Links == * [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] * [https://www.sekigane-yurari.com Tottori Sekigane Onsen Yurarī] {{Tottori Prefecture Hot Springs}} {{citation needed|せきかねおんせん}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kurayoshi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] [[Category:Pages that attribution was given to on Feb 22]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sekigane Onsen]] [[ja:関金温泉]] [[ja:有馬温泉]] [[ja:増富温泉]] [[ja:玉川温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ja:母畑温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sekigane Onsen}}

According to the Genkō-era version, the lord of Sekigane Castle, Yamana Kotarō, is said to have opened the springYamana Kotarō was a member of the Yamana clan, guardians of Hōki, who was said to have been killed in battle at Sekigane during a succession dispute. However, this incident likely occurred in the late 15th century, which is significantly later than the Genkō era (mid-14th century).. Additionally, early Showa-era literature states that Gyōki (668–749) discovered the spring, and after a period of decline, Kūkai (774–835) revived it, with Yamana Kotarō (14??–1489?) installing the bath.. Sekigane Onsen is also known by the ancient names "Ginnoyu" and "Shiroganeyu" due to the beauty of its waters, and it appears as "Ginyu" in the Hōki Mindanki. During the Edo period, it was located along the 【Binchū Kaidō】 (also known as the Mimasaka Kaidō) connecting central Tottori with the Sanyō region, and a post station, Sekigane-juku, was established there. Prior to the era when radiation was measured, the water was described as a pale yellow, odorless carbonated spring. According to surveys conducted by the Tottori Prefecture based on the admission tax, the number of users peaked at around 34,000 in 2003 (Heisei 15). Usage then declined to approximately 13,000 in 2012, but has since rebounded to around 23,000 in 2017. Estimates based on municipal reports prior to 1997 (Heisei 9) suggest that during the bubble economy, annual usage might have been around 400,000 visitors. == Events and Festivals == In this region, traditional customs such as the imperial procession on October 7 and rice-planting songs (denoted in the "Daisenji Engi Emaki") are observed. * The "Sekigane Satomi Festival" is held on the first Sunday of September. == Nearby Attractions == * '''[[Daikatsuyama Jizōin (Kurayoshi)|Daikatsuyama Jizōin]]''' – Historically known as "Sekino Jizō" in Sekigane Onsen. The wooden half-seated Jizō Bosatsu statue here is designated as a nationally important cultural property, indicating that the Jizōin was established in 1192 (Kenkyu 3) by Sasaki Takatsuna. (Note that the current structure is a reconstruction.) Other cultural assets, such as a Giboju and a Song dynasty celadon incense burner, are also preserved. * {{ill|Yukase Shrine|ja|湯関神社|12=draft}} * The ruins of {{ill|Kamei Castle|ja|亀井城跡|12=draft}} == Access == * By Car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft}} toward Kurayoshi. * By Rail and Bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|12=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|12=draft}} for about 35 minutes. : Previously, a branch of the {{ill|Kurayoshi Line|ja|倉吉線|12=draft}} served Sekigane Station as the nearest stop, but the line was abolished on April 1, 1985 (Showa 60) and the station was removed. : Although bus schedules and frequencies are limited, it is also possible to access the area by taking a community bus ("Maniwakun") on the 37 "Harsan-Kūse Route" from the {{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|12=draft}}/{{ill|Chūgoku Katsuya Station|ja|中国勝山駅|12=draft}}, transferring at Miyata Stop onto the 32 "Chūso-Sekigane Route." * By Highway Bus: Services such as the {{ill|Shin-Kurayoshi Kaidō Express|ja|新倉吉街道エクスプレス|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Meribird-go (Hiroshima - Tottori Line)|ja|メリーバード号 (広島 - 鳥取線)|12=draft}} operate. == Footnotes and References == {{Reflist |refs= *[http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf Tottori Prefecture Cultural Tourism Bureau, Tourism Policy Division: Visitor Dynamics Survey (Heisei 22)] (accessed September 9, 2014). * [http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226511/3003onsenirikomi.pdf Tottori Prefecture Tourism Exchange Bureau, Tourism Strategy Division: Hot Spring Visitor Numbers (March, Heisei 30)] (accessed June 7, 2018). *『全国温泉大事典』, pp.560–563 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』, pp.492–500 "Sekigane-machi" *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（角川日本地名大辞典）』, p.436 "Sekigane Onsen" *『全國温泉案内』, pp.443–453 *『溫泉案内』, pp.216–227 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』, pp.334–335 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』, pp.251–252 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』, pp.158–160 *[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財等データベース: Wooden Jizō Bosatsu Half-seated Statue] (accessed September 25, 2014). }} == See Also == * [[Tourist Attractions in Tottori Prefecture]] * [[Misaso Onsen]] – Another radioactive hot spring nearby == External Links == * [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] * [https://www.sekigane-yurari.com Tottori Sekigane Onsen Yurarī] {{Tottori Prefecture Hot Springs}} {{citation needed|せきかねおんせん}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kurayoshi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] [[Category:Pages that attribution was given to on Feb 22]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sekigane Onsen]] [[ja:関金温泉]] [[ja:有馬温泉]] [[ja:増富温泉]] [[ja:玉川温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ja:母畑温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sekigane Onsen}}

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Sekigane Onsen |image = File:Sekigane onsen Yumeikan.jpg |Comment = Sekigane Onsen Yumemyōkan |Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 21 |Latitude Seconds = 44.97 |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 45 |Longitude Seconds = 46.21 |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|12=draft}} Sekigane-machi, Sekigane-yado |Access = : By car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原IC|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft}} toward Kurayoshi
: By rail and local bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|12=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|12=draft}} for about 35 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Radiation Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft}} |Temperature = 40 - 62 |Discharge = 337 L/min |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 5 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 23,410 |Statistical Year = 2017 |External links = [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] |Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 21 | lat_sec = 44.97 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 45 | lon_sec = 46.21 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Sekigane Onsen | position = bottom | mark = Map pointer.svg | marksize = 9 | width = 300 | float = right | caption = Location of Sekigane Onsen | alt = Location of Sekigane Onsen }} '''Sekigane Onsen''' (せきがねおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|12=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft}}). == Water Quality == It is a {{ill|radiation hot spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft}}, ranking second in Japan after {{ill|Misaso Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=draft}} in its content of {{ill|radon|ja|ラドン|12=draft}} and other radioactive elements. Its water is colorless and odorless, renowned as a "beautiful hot spring." Although the reason why both Sekigane Onsen and Misaso Onsen exhibit radioactivity was long unknown, it was determined in 1955 (Showa 30) that a water-formed {{ill|uranium|ja|ウラン|12=draft}} mineral deposit discovered nearby is the source. The source temperature is between 40 and 62℃. == Onsen Town == {{File:Sekigane onsen YURARI.jpg|thumb|Sekigane Onsen Yurarī}} The hot spring town extends along the eastern foothills of {{ill|Daisen|ja|大山|12=draft}}, located deep from {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft}}. There are five ryokan in the area. One public bath, "Kan no Yu," is available, and as a day-use facility, "Sekigane Yumemyōkan" is also in operation. The town comprises both an old onsen district and a new district developed in 1963 (Showa 38) that draws water from a municipal source. When the area was designated as a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}} on March 24, 1969 (Showa 45), local regulations were enacted to centralize management of the hot spring sources. An old national lodging facility, Green School Sekigane, once existed here. It has been renovated and reopened as {{ill|HOTEL Hoshitori Terrace Sekigane|ja|HOTEL星取テラスせきがね|12=draft}} on April 26, 2025 (Reiwa 7)[https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/717807 Kurayoshi's old national lodging renewed; Sekigane hotel to open on April 26 with universal design adoption] (Shanin Chūō Shinpo) January 24, 2025.. == History == Various legends exist regarding the founding of the hot spring. Some sources attribute its opening to the Yōrō era (717–723 AD), others to the Enryaku era (782–806 AD) as noted in the Japanese Hot Spring Chronicles, while still others attribute it to the 12th year of the Jōgan era (870 AD) or even to the Genkō era (1331–1333 AD). One legend recounts that when a crane was seen bathing, the monk 【行基】 discovered the spring, and 【Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)】 later developed the desolate hot spring area. Some accounts even credit the discovery to Kūkai. According to the Genkō-era version, the lord of Sekigane Castle, Yamana Kotarō, is said to have opened the springYamana Kotarō was a member of the Yamana clan, guardians of Hōki, who was said to have been killed in battle at Sekigane during a succession dispute. However, this incident likely occurred in the late 15th century, which is significantly later than the Genkō era (mid-14th century).. Additionally, early Showa-era literature states that Gyōki (668–749) discovered the spring, and after a period of decline, Kūkai (774–835) revived it, with Yamana Kotarō (14??–1489?) installing the bath.. Sekigane Onsen is also known by the ancient names "Ginnoyu" and "Shiroganeyu" due to the beauty of its waters, and it appears as "Ginyu" in the Hōki Mindanki. During the Edo period, it was located along the 【Binchū Kaidō】 (also known as the Mimasaka Kaidō) connecting central Tottori with the Sanyō region, and a post station, Sekigane-juku, was established there. Prior to the era when radiation was measured, the water was described as a pale yellow, odorless carbonated spring. According to surveys conducted by the Tottori Prefecture based on the admission tax, the number of users peaked at around 34,000 in 2003 (Heisei 15). Usage then declined to approximately 13,000 in 2012, but has since rebounded to around 23,000 in 2017. Estimates based on municipal reports prior to 1997 (Heisei 9) suggest that during the bubble economy, annual usage might have been around 400,000 visitors. == Events and Festivals == In this region, traditional customs such as the imperial procession on October 7 and rice-planting songs (denoted in the "Daisenji Engi Emaki") are observed. * The "Sekigane Satomi Festival" is held on the first Sunday of September. == Nearby Attractions == * '''[[Daikatsuyama Jizōin (Kurayoshi)|Daikatsuyama Jizōin]]''' – Historically known as "Sekino Jizō" in Sekigane Onsen. The wooden half-seated Jizō Bosatsu statue here is designated as a nationally important cultural property, indicating that the Jizōin was established in 1192 (Kenkyu 3) by Sasaki Takatsuna. (Note that the current structure is a reconstruction.) Other cultural assets, such as a Giboju and a Song dynasty celadon incense burner, are also preserved. * {{ill|Yukase Shrine|ja|湯関神社|12=draft}} * The ruins of {{ill|Kamei Castle|ja|亀井城跡|12=draft}} == Access == * By Car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft}} toward Kurayoshi. * By Rail and Bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|12=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|12=draft}} for about 35 minutes. : Previously, a branch of the {{ill|Kurayoshi Line|ja|倉吉線|12=draft}} served Sekigane Station as the nearest stop, but the line was abolished on April 1, 1985 (Showa 60) and the station was removed. : Although bus schedules and frequencies are limited, it is also possible to access the area by taking a community bus ("Maniwakun") on the 37 "Harsan-Kūse Route" from the {{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|12=draft}}/{{ill|Chūgoku Katsuya Station|ja|中国勝山駅|12=draft}}, transferring at Miyata Stop onto the 32 "Chūso-Sekigane Route." * By Highway Bus: Services such as the {{ill|Shin-Kurayoshi Kaidō Express|ja|新倉吉街道エクスプレス|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Meribird-go (Hiroshima - Tottori Line)|ja|メリーバード号 (広島 - 鳥取線)|12=draft}} operate. == Footnotes and References == {{Reflist |refs= *[http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf Tottori Prefecture Cultural Tourism Bureau, Tourism Policy Division: Visitor Dynamics Survey (Heisei 22)] (accessed September 9, 2014). * [http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226511/3003onsenirikomi.pdf Tottori Prefecture Tourism Exchange Bureau, Tourism Strategy Division: Hot Spring Visitor Numbers (March, Heisei 30)] (accessed June 7, 2018). *『全国温泉大事典』, pp.560–563 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』, pp.492–500 "Sekigane-machi" *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（角川日本地名大辞典）』, p.436 "Sekigane Onsen" *『全國温泉案内』, pp.443–453 *『溫泉案内』, pp.216–227 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』, pp.334–335 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』, pp.251–252 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』, pp.158–160 *[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財等データベース: Wooden Jizō Bosatsu Half-seated Statue] (accessed September 25, 2014). }} == See Also == * [[Tourist Attractions in Tottori Prefecture]] * [[Misaso Onsen]] – Another radioactive hot spring nearby == External Links == * [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] * [https://www.sekigane-yurari.com Tottori Sekigane Onsen Yurarī] {{Tottori Prefecture Hot Springs}} {{citation needed|せきかねおんせん}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kurayoshi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] [[Category:Pages that attribution was given to on Feb 22]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sekigane Onsen]] [[ja:関金温泉]] [[ja:有馬温泉]] [[ja:増富温泉]] [[ja:玉川温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ja:母畑温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sekigane Onsen}}

revision.text:
{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 関金温泉 |画像 = File:Sekigane onsen Yumeikan.jpg |コメント = 関金温泉湯命館 |緯度度=35|緯度分=21|緯度秒=44.97 |経度度=133|経度分=45|経度秒=46.21 |所在地 = [[鳥取県]][[倉吉市]]関金町関金宿 |交通アクセス = 車：[[米子自動車道]][[湯原インターチェンジ|湯原IC]]から[[国道313号]]を倉吉方面へ約35分
鉄道・路線バス：[[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[山陰本線]][[倉吉駅]]より、[[日本交通 (鳥取)|日交バス]]で約35分 |泉質 = [[放射能泉]] |泉温 = 40 - 62 |湧出量 = 毎分337リットル |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 5 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = 23,410 |統計年度 = 2017 |外部リンク = [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ 倉吉観光マイス協会　倉吉観光情報] |特記事項 = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 21 | lat_sec = 44.97 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 45 | lon_sec = 46.21 | lon_dir = E | label = 関金温泉の位置 | position = bottom | mark = Map pointer.svg | marksize = 9 | width = 300 | float = right | caption = 関金温泉の位置 | alt = 関金温泉の位置 }} '''関金温泉'''（せきがねおんせん）は、[[鳥取県]][[倉吉市]]（旧国[[伯耆国]]）にある[[温泉]]。 == 泉質 == [[放射能泉]]で、[[三朝温泉]]に次いで日本国内第2位の[[ラドン]][[放射能]]を有する。無色無臭の美湯。関金温泉と三朝温泉の湯が放射能を帯びている理由は長年不明だったが、[[1955年]]（昭和30年）に近くで水成[[ウラン]]鉱が発見され、これに由来することが判明した。 源泉温度は40 - 62℃である。 == 温泉街 == [[ファイル:Sekigane onsen YURARI.jpg|サムネイル|関金温泉湯楽里]] [[大山 (鳥取県)|大山]]の東麓、[[国道313号]]から奥に入った場所に温泉街が広がる。[[旅館]]は5軒存在する。[[共同浴場]]は1軒、「関の湯」が存在する。また[[日帰り入浴施設]]として「せきがね湯命館」も存在する。 旧来の温泉街区と[[1963年]]（昭和38年）に開削された町営の源泉を引く新温泉区があるが、[[1969年]]（昭和45年）[[3月24日]]に[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定されると、町では条例を定めて源泉を集中管理するようになった。 旧[[国民宿舎]]グリーンスコーレせきがねがあったが、[[2025年]]（令和7年）[[4月26日]]に「[[HOTEL星取テラスせきがね]]」としてリニューアルオープンすることとなった[https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/717807 倉吉の旧国民宿舎リニューアル 関金のホテル、4月26日開業 ユニバーサルデザイン採用]（山陰中央新報）2025年1月24日。。 == 歴史 == [[開湯伝説]]は諸説ある。開湯年代を[[養老|養老年間]]（西暦[[717年|717]]-[[723年]]）とするもの、[[延暦|延暦年間]]（[[782年]]-[[806年]]）とするもの（『日本鉱泉誌』）、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]12年（[[870年]]）とするもの、[[元弘|元弘年間]]（[[1331年|1331]]-[[1333年]]）とするものがある。 鶴が入浴しているところを[[行基]]が発見し、[[空海|弘法大師]]が荒れ果てていた温泉地を整備したと伝えられる。発見者を弘法大師とする説もある。元弘年間の開湯説では、'''関金城'''主の[[山名小太郎]]が開湯したことになっている山名小太郎は[[伯耆]][[守護]]の[[山名氏]]の一門で、後継者争いに巻き込まれて関金で討ち死にしたと伝えられている。ただしその時期は15世紀末頃であり、元弘年間（14世紀半ば）とは大きく異なる。）。また、昭和初期の文献では、行基（[[668年|668]]-[[749年]]）が発見し、一度廃れたが弘法大師（[[774年|774]]-[[835年]]）が再興し、山名小太郎（14??-1489?年）が浴槽を設置した、となっている。 関金温泉には、湯の美しさから「銀の湯」「しろがねの湯」との古名があり、[[伯耆民談記]]にも「銀湯」として登場する。 江戸時代には、鳥取県中部と山陽地方を結ぶ[[備中街道]]（[[美作街道]]）上にあり、関金宿が置かれていた。[[放射線]]が測定されるより以前の時代には「淡黄色の無臭の炭酸泉」とされている。 鳥取県が入湯税を基に算出した温泉利用者数をみると、ピークは[[2003年]]（平成15年）頃で約34,000人が利用している。その後低迷し[[2012年]]には約13,000人となったが、これを底に再び増加傾向となり[[2017年]]には約23,000人となっている。[[1997年]]（平成9年）以前は利用者の算出方法が異なり、各自治体の申告数に基づく推計値だが、これに遵うと[[バブル景気]]の頃には年間40万人前後の利用客があった計算になる。 == 行事・祭事 == この地域では、[[10月7日]]の御幸行列や、『大山寺縁起絵巻』にも描かれている田植歌（田植田楽）の習俗を持つ。 * [[せきがね里見まつり]] - 9月第一日曜日開催 ==付近の名所== * '''[[地蔵院 (倉吉市)|大滝山地蔵院]]''' - 古くから関金温泉で「関の地蔵」と呼ばれてきた。地蔵院の菩薩像「'''木造地蔵菩薩半跏像'''」は国指定の[[重要文化財]]で、地蔵院が[[1192年]]（[[建久3年]]）に[[佐々木高綱]]によって建立されたことを示している。（ただし、現存するものは再建されたものである。）ほかに、県指定の文化財として[[擬宝珠]]や[[青磁|宋青磁香炉]]が伝わる。 * 湯関神社 * 亀井城跡 == アクセス == * 自動車：[[米子自動車道]][[湯原インターチェンジ|湯原IC]]から[[国道313号]]を倉吉方面へ約35分。 * 鉄道・路線バス：[[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[山陰本線]][[倉吉駅]]より、[[日本交通 (鳥取)|日交バス]]で約35分。 :かつては倉吉駅から[[倉吉線]]が分岐し、途中の[[関金駅]]が最寄り駅になっていたが、[[1985年]]（昭和60年）[[4月1日]]に倉吉線が廃止され、駅も消滅した。 :運行曜日・便数が限られるが、[[姫新線]][[中国勝山駅]]より真庭市コミュニティバス「まにわくん」の　37 蒜山・久世ルートに乗車し、宮田停留所で 32 中曽・関金ルートに乗り継ぐことでも来訪可能。 * 高速バス：[[新倉吉街道エクスプレス]]、[[メリーバード号 (広島 - 鳥取線)|メリーバード号]] ==脚注== ===参考文献=== *『全國温泉案内』,溫泉研究會,日本書院,1924 *『溫泉案内』,鉄道省,博文館,1940 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』,浅香幸雄・編,小学館,1962 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』講談社,1970 *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（[[角川日本地名大辞典]]）』,角川書店,1982,ISBN 978-4040013107 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』,平凡社,1992 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』,鳥取県歴史散歩研究会・編,山川出版社,1994,2003 *『全国温泉大事典』野口冬人・著,旅行読売出版社,1997,p560-563 *[https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf 鳥取県文化観光局観光政策課 平成22年観光客入込動態調査結果] ===注釈=== ===出典=== {{Reflist |refs= *[https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf 鳥取県文化観光局観光政策課 観光客入込動態調査結果（平成22年）]2014年9月9日閲覧。 * [https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226511/3003onsenirikomi.pdf 鳥取県観光交流局観光戦略課 温泉地入湯客数（平成30年3月）]2018年6月7日閲覧。 *『全国温泉大事典』p560-563 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』p492-500「関金町」 *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（角川日本地名大辞典）』p436「関金温泉」 *『全國温泉案内』p443-453 *『溫泉案内』p216-227 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』p334-335 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』p251-252 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』p158-160 *『全国温泉大事典』p560-563 *[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財等データベース 木造地蔵菩薩半跏像]2014年9月25日閲覧。 }} == 関連項目 == *[[鳥取県の観光地]] *[[三朝温泉]]-近傍にある同じ放射能泉 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.yumeikan.com せきがね湯命館] * [https://www.sekigane-yurari.com 鳥取県関金温泉 湯楽里] {{鳥取県の温泉}} {{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せきかねおんせん}} [[Category:鳥取県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:倉吉市の地理]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Sekigane Onsen |image = File:Sekigane onsen Yumeikan.jpg |Comment = Sekigane Onsen Yumemyōkan |Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 21 |Latitude Seconds = 44.97 |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 45 |Longitude Seconds = 46.21 |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|12=draft|12=draft}} Sekigane-machi, Sekigane-yado |Access = : By car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原IC|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft|12=draft}} toward Kurayoshi
: By rail and local bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 35 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Radiation Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft|12=draft}} |Temperature = 40 - 62 |Discharge = 337 L/min |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 5 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 23,410 |Statistical Year = 2017 |External links = [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] |Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 21 | lat_sec = 44.97 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 45 | lon_sec = 46.21 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Sekigane Onsen | position = bottom | mark = Map pointer.svg | marksize = 9 | width = 300 | float = right | caption = Location of Sekigane Onsen | alt = Location of Sekigane Onsen }} '''Sekigane Onsen''' (せきがねおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Water Quality == It is a {{ill|radiation hot spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, ranking second in Japan after {{ill|Misaso Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} in its content of {{ill|radon|ja|ラドン|12=draft|12=draft}} and other radioactive elements. Its water is colorless and odorless, renowned as a "beautiful hot spring." Although the reason why both Sekigane Onsen and Misaso Onsen exhibit radioactivity was long unknown, it was determined in 1955 (Showa 30) that a water-formed {{ill|uranium|ja|ウラン|12=draft|12=draft}} mineral deposit discovered nearby is the source. The source temperature is between 40 and 62℃. == Onsen Town == {{File:Sekigane onsen YURARI.jpg|thumb|Sekigane Onsen Yurarī}} The hot spring town extends along the eastern foothills of {{ill|Daisen|ja|大山|12=draft|12=draft}}, located deep from {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are five ryokan in the area. One public bath, "Kan no Yu," is available, and as a day-use facility, "Sekigane Yumemyōkan" is also in operation. The town comprises both an old onsen district and a new district developed in 1963 (Showa 38) that draws water from a municipal source. When the area was designated as a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft|12=draft}} on March 24, 1969 (Showa 45), local regulations were enacted to centralize management of the hot spring sources. An old national lodging facility, Green School Sekigane, once existed here. It has been renovated and reopened as {{ill|HOTEL Hoshitori Terrace Sekigane|ja|HOTEL星取テラスせきがね|12=draft|12=draft}} on April 26, 2025 (Reiwa 7)[https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/717807 Kurayoshi's old national lodging renewed; Sekigane hotel to open on April 26 with universal design adoption] (Shanin Chūō Shinpo) January 24, 2025.. == History == Various legends exist regarding the founding of the hot spring. Some sources attribute its opening to the Yōrō era (717–723 AD), others to the Enryaku era (782–806 AD) as noted in the Japanese Hot Spring Chronicles, while still others attribute it to the 12th year of the Jōgan era (870 AD) or even to the Genkō era (1331–1333 AD). One legend recounts that when a crane was seen bathing, the monk 【行基】 discovered the spring, and 【Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)】 later developed the desolate hot spring area. Some accounts even credit the discovery to Kūkai. According to the Genkō-era version, the lord of Sekigane Castle, Yamana Kotarō, is said to have opened the springYamana Kotarō was a member of the Yamana clan, guardians of Hōki, who was said to have been killed in battle at Sekigane during a succession dispute. However, this incident likely occurred in the late 15th century, which is significantly later than the Genkō era (mid-14th century).. Additionally, early Showa-era literature states that Gyōki (668–749) discovered the spring, and after a period of decline, Kūkai (774–835) revived it, with Yamana Kotarō (14??–1489?) installing the bath.. Sekigane Onsen is also known by the ancient names "Ginnoyu" and "Shiroganeyu" due to the beauty of its waters, and it appears as "Ginyu" in the Hōki Mindanki. During the Edo period, it was located along the 【Binchū Kaidō】 (also known as the Mimasaka Kaidō) connecting central Tottori with the Sanyō region, and a post station, Sekigane-juku, was established there. Prior to the era when radiation was measured, the water was described as a pale yellow, odorless carbonated spring. According to surveys conducted by the Tottori Prefecture based on the admission tax, the number of users peaked at around 34,000 in 2003 (Heisei 15). Usage then declined to approximately 13,000 in 2012, but has since rebounded to around 23,000 in 2017. Estimates based on municipal reports prior to 1997 (Heisei 9) suggest that during the bubble economy, annual usage might have been around 400,000 visitors. == Events and Festivals == In this region, traditional customs such as the imperial procession on October 7 and rice-planting songs (denoted in the "Daisenji Engi Emaki") are observed. * The "Sekigane Satomi Festival" is held on the first Sunday of September. == Nearby Attractions == * '''[[Daikatsuyama Jizōin (Kurayoshi)|Daikatsuyama Jizōin]]''' – Historically known as "Sekino Jizō" in Sekigane Onsen. The wooden half-seated Jizō Bosatsu statue here is designated as a nationally important cultural property, indicating that the Jizōin was established in 1192 (Kenkyu 3) by Sasaki Takatsuna. (Note that the current structure is a reconstruction.) Other cultural assets, such as a Giboju and a Song dynasty celadon incense burner, are also preserved. * {{ill|Yukase Shrine|ja|湯関神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * The ruins of {{ill|Kamei Castle|ja|亀井城跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * By Car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft|12=draft}} toward Kurayoshi. * By Rail and Bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 35 minutes. : Previously, a branch of the {{ill|Kurayoshi Line|ja|倉吉線|12=draft|12=draft}} served Sekigane Station as the nearest stop, but the line was abolished on April 1, 1985 (Showa 60) and the station was removed. : Although bus schedules and frequencies are limited, it is also possible to access the area by taking a community bus ("Maniwakun") on the 37 "Harsan-Kūse Route" from the {{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|12=draft|12=draft}}/{{ill|Chūgoku Katsuya Station|ja|中国勝山駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, transferring at Miyata Stop onto the 32 "Chūso-Sekigane Route." * By Highway Bus: Services such as the {{ill|Shin-Kurayoshi Kaidō Express|ja|新倉吉街道エクスプレス|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Meribird-go (Hiroshima - Tottori Line)|ja|メリーバード号 (広島 - 鳥取線)|12=draft|12=draft}} operate. == Footnotes and References == {{Reflist |refs= *[http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf Tottori Prefecture Cultural Tourism Bureau, Tourism Policy Division: Visitor Dynamics Survey (Heisei 22)] (accessed September 9, 2014). * [http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226511/3003onsenirikomi.pdf Tottori Prefecture Tourism Exchange Bureau, Tourism Strategy Division: Hot Spring Visitor Numbers (March, Heisei 30)] (accessed June 7, 2018). *『全国温泉大事典』, pp.560–563 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』, pp.492–500 "Sekigane-machi" *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（角川日本地名大辞典）』, p.436 "Sekigane Onsen" *『全國温泉案内』, pp.443–453 *『溫泉案内』, pp.216–227 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』, pp.334–335 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』, pp.251–252 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』, pp.158–160 *[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財等データベース: Wooden Jizō Bosatsu Half-seated Statue] (accessed September 25, 2014). }} == See Also == * [[Tourist Attractions in Tottori Prefecture]] * [[Misaso Onsen]] – Another radioactive hot spring nearby == External Links == * [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] * [https://www.sekigane-yurari.com Tottori Sekigane Onsen Yurarī] {{Tottori Prefecture Hot Springs}} {{citation needed|せきかねおんせん}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kurayoshi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] [[Category:Pages that attribution was given to on Feb 22]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sekigane Onsen]] [[ja:関金温泉]] [[ja:有馬温泉]] [[ja:増富温泉]] [[ja:玉川温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ja:母畑温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sekigane Onsen}}

One legend recounts that when a crane was seen bathing, the monk 【行基】 discovered the spring, and 【Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)】 later developed the desolate hot spring area. Some accounts even credit the discovery to Kūkai. According to the Genkō-era version, the lord of Sekigane Castle, Yamana Kotarō, is said to have opened the springYamana Kotarō was a member of the Yamana clan, guardians of Hōki, who was said to have been killed in battle at Sekigane during a succession dispute. However, this incident likely occurred in the late 15th century, which is significantly later than the Genkō era (mid-14th century).. Additionally, early Showa-era literature states that Gyōki (668–749) discovered the spring, and after a period of decline, Kūkai (774–835) revived it, with Yamana Kotarō (14??–1489?) installing the bath.. Sekigane Onsen is also known by the ancient names "Ginnoyu" and "Shiroganeyu" due to the beauty of its waters, and it appears as "Ginyu" in the Hōki Mindanki. During the Edo period, it was located along the 【Binchū Kaidō】 (also known as the Mimasaka Kaidō) connecting central Tottori with the Sanyō region, and a post station, Sekigane-juku, was established there. Prior to the era when radiation was measured, the water was described as a pale yellow, odorless carbonated spring. According to surveys conducted by the Tottori Prefecture based on the admission tax, the number of users peaked at around 34,000 in 2003 (Heisei 15). Usage then declined to approximately 13,000 in 2012, but has since rebounded to around 23,000 in 2017. Estimates based on municipal reports prior to 1997 (Heisei 9) suggest that during the bubble economy, annual usage might have been around 400,000 visitors. == Events and Festivals == In this region, traditional customs such as the imperial procession on October 7 and rice-planting songs (denoted in the "Daisenji Engi Emaki") are observed. * The "Sekigane Satomi Festival" is held on the first Sunday of September. == Nearby Attractions == * '''[[Daikatsuyama Jizōin (Kurayoshi)|Daikatsuyama Jizōin]]''' – Historically known as "Sekino Jizō" in Sekigane Onsen. The wooden half-seated Jizō Bosatsu statue here is designated as a nationally important cultural property, indicating that the Jizōin was established in 1192 (Kenkyu 3) by Sasaki Takatsuna. (Note that the current structure is a reconstruction.) Other cultural assets, such as a Giboju and a Song dynasty celadon incense burner, are also preserved. * {{ill|Yukase Shrine|ja|湯関神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * The ruins of {{ill|Kamei Castle|ja|亀井城跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * By Car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft|12=draft}} toward Kurayoshi. * By Rail and Bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 35 minutes. : Previously, a branch of the {{ill|Kurayoshi Line|ja|倉吉線|12=draft|12=draft}} served Sekigane Station as the nearest stop, but the line was abolished on April 1, 1985 (Showa 60) and the station was removed. : Although bus schedules and frequencies are limited, it is also possible to access the area by taking a community bus ("Maniwakun") on the 37 "Harsan-Kūse Route" from the {{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|12=draft|12=draft}}/{{ill|Chūgoku Katsuya Station|ja|中国勝山駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, transferring at Miyata Stop onto the 32 "Chūso-Sekigane Route." * By Highway Bus: Services such as the {{ill|Shin-Kurayoshi Kaidō Express|ja|新倉吉街道エクスプレス|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Meribird-go (Hiroshima - Tottori Line)|ja|メリーバード号 (広島 - 鳥取線)|12=draft|12=draft}} operate. == Footnotes and References == {{Reflist |refs= *[http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf Tottori Prefecture Cultural Tourism Bureau, Tourism Policy Division: Visitor Dynamics Survey (Heisei 22)] (accessed September 9, 2014). * [http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226511/3003onsenirikomi.pdf Tottori Prefecture Tourism Exchange Bureau, Tourism Strategy Division: Hot Spring Visitor Numbers (March, Heisei 30)] (accessed June 7, 2018). *『全国温泉大事典』, pp.560–563 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』, pp.492–500 "Sekigane-machi" *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（角川日本地名大辞典）』, p.436 "Sekigane Onsen" *『全國温泉案内』, pp.443–453 *『溫泉案内』, pp.216–227 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』, pp.334–335 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』, pp.251–252 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』, pp.158–160 *[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財等データベース: Wooden Jizō Bosatsu Half-seated Statue] (accessed September 25, 2014). }} == See Also == * [[Tourist Attractions in Tottori Prefecture]] * [[Misaso Onsen]] – Another radioactive hot spring nearby == External Links == * [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] * [https://www.sekigane-yurari.com Tottori Sekigane Onsen Yurarī] {{Tottori Prefecture Hot Springs}} {{citation needed|せきかねおんせん}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kurayoshi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sekigane Onsen]] [[ja:関金温泉]] [[ja:有馬温泉]] [[ja:増富温泉]] [[ja:玉川温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ja:母畑温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sekigane Onsen}}

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Sekigane Onsen |image = File:Sekigane onsen Yumeikan.jpg |Comment = Sekigane Onsen Yumemyōkan |Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 21 |Latitude Seconds = 44.97 |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 45 |Longitude Seconds = 46.21 |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|12=draft|12=draft}} Sekigane-machi, Sekigane-yado |Access = : By car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原IC|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft|12=draft}} toward Kurayoshi
: By rail and local bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 35 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Radiation Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft|12=draft}} |Temperature = 40 - 62 |Discharge = 337 L/min |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 5 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 23,410 |Statistical Year = 2017 |External links = [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] |Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 21 | lat_sec = 44.97 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 45 | lon_sec = 46.21 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Sekigane Onsen | position = bottom | mark = Map pointer.svg | marksize = 9 | width = 300 | float = right | caption = Location of Sekigane Onsen | alt = Location of Sekigane Onsen }} '''Sekigane Onsen''' (せきがねおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Water Quality == It is a {{ill|radiation hot spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ranking second in Japan after {{ill|Misaso Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} in its content of {{ill|radon|ja|ラドン|12=draft|12=draft}} and other radioactive elements. Its water is colorless and odorless, renowned as a "beautiful hot spring." Although the reason why both Sekigane Onsen and Misaso Onsen exhibit radioactivity was long unknown, it was determined in 1955 (Showa 30) that a water-formed {{ill|uranium|ja|ウラン|12=draft|12=draft}} mineral deposit discovered nearby is the source. The source temperature is between 40 and 62℃. == Onsen Town == {{File:Sekigane onsen YURARI.jpg|thumb|Sekigane Onsen Yurarī}} The hot spring town extends along the eastern foothills of {{ill|Daisen|ja|大山|12=draft|12=draft}}, located deep from {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are five ryokan in the area. One public bath, "Kan no Yu," is available, and as a day-use facility, "Sekigane Yumemyōkan" is also in operation. The town comprises both an old onsen district and a new district developed in 1963 (Showa 38) that draws water from a municipal source. When the area was designated as a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft|12=draft}} on March 24, 1969 (Showa 45), local regulations were enacted to centralize management of the hot spring sources. An old national lodging facility, Green School Sekigane, once existed here. It has been renovated and reopened as {{ill|HOTEL Hoshitori Terrace Sekigane|ja|HOTEL星取テラスせきがね|12=draft|12=draft}} on April 26, 2025 (Reiwa 7)[https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/717807 Kurayoshi's old national lodging renewed; Sekigane hotel to open on April 26 with universal design adoption] (Shanin Chūō Shinpo) January 24, 2025.. == History == Various legends exist regarding the founding of the hot spring. Some sources attribute its opening to the Yōrō era (717–723 AD), others to the Enryaku era (782–806 AD) as noted in the Japanese Hot Spring Chronicles, while still others attribute it to the 12th year of the Jōgan era (870 AD) or even to the Genkō era (1331–1333 AD). One legend recounts that when a crane was seen bathing, the monk 【行基】 discovered the spring, and 【Kōbō Daishi (Kūkai)】 later developed the desolate hot spring area. Some accounts even credit the discovery to Kūkai. According to the Genkō-era version, the lord of Sekigane Castle, Yamana Kotarō, is said to have opened the springYamana Kotarō was a member of the Yamana clan, guardians of Hōki, who was said to have been killed in battle at Sekigane during a succession dispute. However, this incident likely occurred in the late 15th century, which is significantly later than the Genkō era (mid-14th century).. Additionally, early Showa-era literature states that Gyōki (668–749) discovered the spring, and after a period of decline, Kūkai (774–835) revived it, with Yamana Kotarō (14??–1489?) installing the bath.. Sekigane Onsen is also known by the ancient names "Ginnoyu" and "Shiroganeyu" due to the beauty of its waters, and it appears as "Ginyu" in the Hōki Mindanki. During the Edo period, it was located along the 【Binchū Kaidō】 (also known as the Mimasaka Kaidō) connecting central Tottori with the Sanyō region, and a post station, Sekigane-juku, was established there. Prior to the era when radiation was measured, the water was described as a pale yellow, odorless carbonated spring. According to surveys conducted by the Tottori Prefecture based on the admission tax, the number of users peaked at around 34,000 in 2003 (Heisei 15). Usage then declined to approximately 13,000 in 2012, but has since rebounded to around 23,000 in 2017. Estimates based on municipal reports prior to 1997 (Heisei 9) suggest that during the bubble economy, annual usage might have been around 400,000 visitors. == Events and Festivals == In this region, traditional customs such as the imperial procession on October 7 and rice-planting songs (denoted in the "Daisenji Engi Emaki") are observed. * The "Sekigane Satomi Festival" is held on the first Sunday of September. == Nearby Attractions == * '''[[Daikatsuyama Jizōin (Kurayoshi)|Daikatsuyama Jizōin]]''' – Historically known as "Sekino Jizō" in Sekigane Onsen. The wooden half-seated Jizō Bosatsu statue here is designated as a nationally important cultural property, indicating that the Jizōin was established in 1192 (Kenkyu 3) by Sasaki Takatsuna. (Note that the current structure is a reconstruction.) Other cultural assets, such as a Giboju and a Song dynasty celadon incense burner, are also preserved. * {{ill|Yukase Shrine|ja|湯関神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * The ruins of {{ill|Kamei Castle|ja|亀井城跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * By Car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|12=draft|12=draft}} toward Kurayoshi. * By Rail and Bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 35 minutes. : Previously, a branch of the {{ill|Kurayoshi Line|ja|倉吉線|12=draft|12=draft}} served Sekigane Station as the nearest stop, but the line was abolished on April 1, 1985 (Showa 60) and the station was removed. : Although bus schedules and frequencies are limited, it is also possible to access the area by taking a community bus ("Maniwakun") on the 37 "Harsan-Kūse Route" from the {{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|12=draft|12=draft}}/{{ill|Chūgoku Katsuya Station|ja|中国勝山駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, transferring at Miyata Stop onto the 32 "Chūso-Sekigane Route." * By Highway Bus: Services such as the {{ill|Shin-Kurayoshi Kaidō Express|ja|新倉吉街道エクスプレス|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Meribird-go (Hiroshima - Tottori Line)|ja|メリーバード号 (広島 - 鳥取線)|12=draft|12=draft}} operate. == Footnotes and References == {{Reflist |refs= *[http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf Tottori Prefecture Cultural Tourism Bureau, Tourism Policy Division: Visitor Dynamics Survey (Heisei 22)] (accessed September 9, 2014). * [http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226511/3003onsenirikomi.pdf Tottori Prefecture Tourism Exchange Bureau, Tourism Strategy Division: Hot Spring Visitor Numbers (March, Heisei 30)] (accessed June 7, 2018). *『全国温泉大事典』, pp.560–563 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』, pp.492–500 "Sekigane-machi" *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（角川日本地名大辞典）』, p.436 "Sekigane Onsen" *『全國温泉案内』, pp.443–453 *『溫泉案内』, pp.216–227 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』, pp.334–335 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』, pp.251–252 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』, pp.158–160 *[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財等データベース: Wooden Jizō Bosatsu Half-seated Statue] (accessed September 25, 2014). }} == See Also == * [[Tourist Attractions in Tottori Prefecture]] * [[Misaso Onsen]] – Another radioactive hot spring nearby == External Links == * [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] * [https://www.sekigane-yurari.com Tottori Sekigane Onsen Yurarī] {{Tottori Prefecture Hot Springs}} {{citation needed|せきかねおんせん}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kurayoshi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sekigane Onsen]] [[ja:関金温泉]] [[ja:有馬温泉]] [[ja:増富温泉]] [[ja:玉川温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ja:母畑温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sekigane Onsen}}

(Note that the current structure is a reconstruction.) Other cultural assets, such as a Giboju and a Song dynasty celadon incense burner, are also preserved. * {{ill|Yukase Shrine|ja|湯関神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * The ruins of {{ill|Kamei Castle|ja|亀井城跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * By Car: Approximately 35 minutes from {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yubaru Interchange|ja|湯原インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 313|ja|国道313号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} toward Kurayoshi. * By Rail and Bus: From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR West|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take a {{ill|Nikkō Kōtsu Bus|ja|日交バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for about 35 minutes. : Previously, a branch of the {{ill|Kurayoshi Line|ja|倉吉線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} served Sekigane Station as the nearest stop, but the line was abolished on April 1, 1985 (Showa 60) and the station was removed. : Although bus schedules and frequencies are limited, it is also possible to access the area by taking a community bus ("Maniwakun") on the 37 "Harsan-Kūse Route" from the {{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}/{{ill|Chūgoku Katsuya Station|ja|中国勝山駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, transferring at Miyata Stop onto the 32 "Chūso-Sekigane Route." * By Highway Bus: Services such as the {{ill|Shin-Kurayoshi Kaidō Express|ja|新倉吉街道エクスプレス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Meribird-go (Hiroshima - Tottori Line)|ja|メリーバード号 (広島 - 鳥取線)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} operate. == Footnotes and References == {{Reflist |refs= *[http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf Tottori Prefecture Cultural Tourism Bureau, Tourism Policy Division: Visitor Dynamics Survey (Heisei 22)] (accessed September 9, 2014). * [http://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226511/3003onsenirikomi.pdf Tottori Prefecture Tourism Exchange Bureau, Tourism Strategy Division: Hot Spring Visitor Numbers (March, Heisei 30)] (accessed June 7, 2018). *『全国温泉大事典』, pp.560–563 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』, pp.492–500 "Sekigane-machi" *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（角川日本地名大辞典）』, p.436 "Sekigane Onsen" *『全國温泉案内』, pp.443–453 *『溫泉案内』, pp.216–227 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』, pp.334–335 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』, pp.251–252 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』, pp.158–160 *[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財等データベース: Wooden Jizō Bosatsu Half-seated Statue] (accessed September 25, 2014). }} == See Also == * [[Tourist Attractions in Tottori Prefecture]] * [[Misaso Onsen]] – Another radioactive hot spring nearby == External Links == * [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ Kurayoshi Tourism Mice Association – Kurayoshi Tourism Information] * [https://www.sekigane-yurari.com Tottori Sekigane Onsen Yurarī] {{Tottori Prefecture Hot Springs}} {{citation needed|せきかねおんせん}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kurayoshi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Radioactive spring]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sekigane Onsen]] [[ja:関金温泉]] [[ja:有馬温泉]] [[ja:増富温泉]] [[ja:玉川温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ja:母畑温泉]] [[ja:猫啼温泉]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sekigane Onsen}}

revision.comment: 66 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:関金温泉 up to rev 104525026
revision.text:
{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 関金温泉 |画像 = File:Sekigane onsen Yumeikan.jpg |コメント = 関金温泉湯命館 |緯度度=35|緯度分=21|緯度秒=44.97 |経度度=133|経度分=45|経度秒=46.21 |所在地 = [[鳥取県]][[倉吉市]]関金町関金宿 |交通アクセス = 車：[[米子自動車道]][[湯原インターチェンジ|湯原IC]]から[[国道313号]]を倉吉方面へ約35分
鉄道・路線バス：[[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[山陰本線]][[倉吉駅]]より、[[日本交通 (鳥取)|日交バス]]で約35分 |泉質 = [[放射能泉]] |泉温 = 40 - 62 |湧出量 = 毎分337リットル |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 5 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = 23,410 |統計年度 = 2017 |外部リンク = [http://www.apionet.or.jp/kankou/ 倉吉観光マイス協会　倉吉観光情報] |特記事項 = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 21 | lat_sec = 44.97 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 45 | lon_sec = 46.21 | lon_dir = E | label = 関金温泉の位置 | position = bottom | mark = Map pointer.svg | marksize = 9 | width = 300 | float = right | caption = 関金温泉の位置 | alt = 関金温泉の位置 }} '''関金温泉'''（せきがねおんせん）は、[[鳥取県]][[倉吉市]]（旧国[[伯耆国]]）にある[[温泉]]。 == 泉質 == [[放射能泉]]で、[[三朝温泉]]に次いで日本国内第2位の[[ラドン]][[放射能]]を有する。無色無臭の美湯。関金温泉と三朝温泉の湯が放射能を帯びている理由は長年不明だったが、[[1955年]]（昭和30年）に近くで水成[[ウラン]]鉱が発見され、これに由来することが判明した。 源泉温度は40 - 62℃である。 == 温泉街 == [[ファイル:Sekigane onsen YURARI.jpg|サムネイル|関金温泉湯楽里]] [[大山 (鳥取県)|大山]]の東麓、[[国道313号]]から奥に入った場所に温泉街が広がる。[[旅館]]は5軒存在する。[[共同浴場]]は1軒、「関の湯」が存在する。また[[日帰り入浴施設]]として「せきがね湯命館」も存在する。 旧来の温泉街区と[[1963年]]（昭和38年）に開削された町営の源泉を引く新温泉区があるが、[[1969年]]（昭和45年）[[3月24日]]に[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定されると、町では条例を定めて源泉を集中管理するようになった。 旧[[国民宿舎]]グリーンスコーレせきがねがあったが、[[2025年]]（令和7年）[[4月26日]]に「[[HOTEL星取テラスせきがね]]」としてリニューアルオープンすることとなった[https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/717807 倉吉の旧国民宿舎リニューアル 関金のホテル、4月26日開業 ユニバーサルデザイン採用]（山陰中央新報）2025年1月24日。。 == 歴史 == [[開湯伝説]]は諸説ある。開湯年代を[[養老|養老年間]]（西暦[[717年|717]]-[[723年]]）とするもの、[[延暦|延暦年間]]（[[782年]]-[[806年]]）とするもの（『日本鉱泉誌』）、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]12年（[[870年]]）とするもの、[[元弘|元弘年間]]（[[1331年|1331]]-[[1333年]]）とするものがある。 鶴が入浴しているところを[[行基]]が発見し、[[空海|弘法大師]]が荒れ果てていた温泉地を整備したと伝えられる。発見者を弘法大師とする説もある。元弘年間の開湯説では、'''関金城'''主の[[山名小太郎]]が開湯したことになっている山名小太郎は[[伯耆]][[守護]]の[[山名氏]]の一門で、後継者争いに巻き込まれて関金で討ち死にしたと伝えられている。ただしその時期は15世紀末頃であり、元弘年間（14世紀半ば）とは大きく異なる。）。また、昭和初期の文献では、行基（[[668年|668]]-[[749年]]）が発見し、一度廃れたが弘法大師（[[774年|774]]-[[835年]]）が再興し、山名小太郎（14??-1489?年）が浴槽を設置した、となっている。 関金温泉には、湯の美しさから「銀の湯」「しろがねの湯」との古名があり、[[伯耆民談記]]にも「銀湯」として登場する。 江戸時代には、鳥取県中部と山陽地方を結ぶ[[備中街道]]（[[美作街道]]）上にあり、関金宿が置かれていた。[[放射線]]が測定されるより以前の時代には「淡黄色の無臭の炭酸泉」とされている。 鳥取県が入湯税を基に算出した温泉利用者数をみると、ピークは[[2003年]]（平成15年）頃で約34,000人が利用している。その後低迷し[[2012年]]には約13,000人となったが、これを底に再び増加傾向となり[[2017年]]には約23,000人となっている。[[1997年]]（平成9年）以前は利用者の算出方法が異なり、各自治体の申告数に基づく推計値だが、これに遵うと[[バブル景気]]の頃には年間40万人前後の利用客があった計算になる。 == 行事・祭事 == この地域では、[[10月7日]]の御幸行列や、『大山寺縁起絵巻』にも描かれている田植歌（田植田楽）の習俗を持つ。 * [[せきがね里見まつり]] - 9月第一日曜日開催 ==付近の名所== * '''[[地蔵院 (倉吉市)|大滝山地蔵院]]''' - 古くから関金温泉で「関の地蔵」と呼ばれてきた。地蔵院の菩薩像「'''木造地蔵菩薩半跏像'''」は国指定の[[重要文化財]]で、地蔵院が[[1192年]]（[[建久3年]]）に[[佐々木高綱]]によって建立されたことを示している。（ただし、現存するものは再建されたものである。）ほかに、県指定の文化財として[[擬宝珠]]や[[青磁|宋青磁香炉]]が伝わる。 * 湯関神社 * 亀井城跡 == アクセス == * 自動車：[[米子自動車道]][[湯原インターチェンジ|湯原IC]]から[[国道313号]]を倉吉方面へ約35分。 * 鉄道・路線バス：[[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[山陰本線]][[倉吉駅]]より、[[日本交通 (鳥取)|日交バス]]で約35分。 :かつては倉吉駅から[[倉吉線]]が分岐し、途中の[[関金駅]]が最寄り駅になっていたが、[[1985年]]（昭和60年）[[4月1日]]に倉吉線が廃止され、駅も消滅した。 :運行曜日・便数が限られるが、[[姫新線]][[中国勝山駅]]より真庭市コミュニティバス「まにわくん」の　37 蒜山・久世ルートに乗車し、宮田停留所で 32 中曽・関金ルートに乗り継ぐことでも来訪可能。 * 高速バス：[[新倉吉街道エクスプレス]]、[[メリーバード号 (広島 - 鳥取線)|メリーバード号]] ==脚注== ===参考文献=== *『全國温泉案内』,溫泉研究會,日本書院,1924 *『溫泉案内』,鉄道省,博文館,1940 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』,浅香幸雄・編,小学館,1962 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』講談社,1970 *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（[[角川日本地名大辞典]]）』,角川書店,1982,ISBN 978-4040013107 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』,平凡社,1992 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』,鳥取県歴史散歩研究会・編,山川出版社,1994,2003 *『全国温泉大事典』野口冬人・著,旅行読売出版社,1997,p560-563 *[https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf 鳥取県文化観光局観光政策課 平成22年観光客入込動態調査結果] ===注釈=== ===出典=== {{Reflist |refs= *[https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226510/h22toukei.pdf 鳥取県文化観光局観光政策課 観光客入込動態調査結果（平成22年）]2014年9月9日閲覧。 * [https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/secure/226511/3003onsenirikomi.pdf 鳥取県観光交流局観光戦略課 温泉地入湯客数（平成30年3月）]2018年6月7日閲覧。 *『全国温泉大事典』p560-563 *『鳥取県の地名（日本歴史地名大系）』p492-500「関金町」 *『日本地名大辞典 31 鳥取県（角川日本地名大辞典）』p436「関金温泉」 *『全國温泉案内』p443-453 *『溫泉案内』p216-227 *『図説日本文化地理大系4 中国1』p334-335 *『日本の文化地理　第13巻 兵庫・岡山・鳥取』p251-252 *『鳥取県の歴史散歩』p158-160 *『全国温泉大事典』p560-563 *[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財等データベース 木造地蔵菩薩半跏像]2014年9月25日閲覧。 }} == 関連項目 == *[[鳥取県の観光地]] *[[三朝温泉]]-近傍にある同じ放射能泉 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.yumeikan.com せきがね湯命館] * [https://www.sekigane-yurari.com 鳥取県関金温泉 湯楽里] {{鳥取県の温泉}} {{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せきかねおんせん}} [[Category:鳥取県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:倉吉市の地理]]
